$comment: Boundary-case fixture for the distributed-boundary matrix. Not a live release packet. See docs/compat/boundary_matrix.md.
scenario: A desktop client targets a managed control plane API family two minors past the declared current_plus_previous_minor_or_lts window. The service refuses privileged writes but retains the cached read-only posture so prior reads remain attributable. The client surfaces the fail-closed reason verbatim and renders cached safe operations only.

supported_skew_window.summary: Current published service family plus the declared previous minor or LTS window. Control-plane additive evolution may move ahead of clients; breaking moves must stage by region or ring.

upgrade_order.notes: Control plane ahead of clients only for additive evolution; breaking moves stage by region or ring before any client update.

rollback_order.notes: Roll control plane back first when a region or ring regresses; clients stay on the cached read-only posture until the rollback completes.

unsupported_state_behavior.contract_rule: Cached read-only safe operations only; privileged writes are blocked with an explicit incompatibility reason.

what_happens_when_upgrade_order_violated: If the managed control plane advances past the published adjacent window before clients are updated, privileged writes refuse with an attributed reason and a staged-rollout hint. The client remains functional for cached read-only operations. Same visible contract applies when the rollout is staged by region and the current client is targeting an un-rolled region.
what_happens_when_skew_window_exceeded: Privileged writes targeting the out-of-window API family fail closed; cached reads remain available where the prior window supported them. The surface renders the declared read-only posture plus a typed upgrade or wait-for-staged-rollout hint. No silent widening to the new family.
notes: Illustrative seed boundary case; this is not a live release packet. The same degrade-to-limited-mode contract also covers boundary_row:provider_linked_packet and boundary_row:browser_companion_and_remote_or_review_session.
